extraction.one_line_summary: GEOGHEGAN HART won stage via 10-second bonus after DENNIS paced INEOS attacks from 51km out, tied HINDLEY on GC
tactical_patterns: DENNIS dictating tempo from 51km neutralized HINDLEY attacks, proving super-domestique pacing trumps solo counterattacks at altitude.
KELDERMAN cracked on first Sestriere ascent 51km out while HINDLEY survived, revealing second-tier GC riders fold earliest multi-lap formats.
INEOS prioritized stage bonus seconds over pure time gaps, GEOGHEGAN HART sprinting for 10-second bonus despite controlling race.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: MVP performance controlling 51km finale suggests peak form enables domestiques to dictate GC battles decisively
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: survived DENNIS pacing but couldn't attack away, signaling time trial weakness forced defensive racing for pink
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: dropped 51km from finish on first lap suggests multi-climb formats expose recovery deficit versus pure climbers
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: attacked for podium after cracking earlier shows recovery capacity exceeded expectations for first Grand Tour
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: dropped immediately when DENNIS accelerated reveals declining form or poor altitude acclimatization third week
